Tragacanth, scientifically known as Astragalus gummifer, is a shrub-like plant native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions, characterized by its ability to produce a unique, non-digestible gum. The health benefits of Tragacanth include soothing digestive issues, such as diarrhea and irritable bowel syndrome, as well as exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Tragacanth include tragacanthic acid, tragacanthin, and other polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tragacanth include lozenges, cough syrups, and ointments for skin conditions.
